title: Grant serviceusage.services.use to debian-repro-ci service account
description:
The GCP service account debian-repro-ci@debian-repro-oxide.iam.gserviceaccount.com lacks the serviceusage.services.use permission, which prevents gcloud builds submit from accessing the Cloud Build staging bucket (debian-repro-oxide_cloudbuild). The auth fix (secrets→vars) is deployed and working; this is the remaining IAM grant needed for GCP builds to succeed.

Error: 'The user is forbidden from accessing the bucket [debian-repro-oxide_cloudbuild]. Please check your organization policy or if the user has the serviceusage.services.use permission.'

Fix: grant roles/serviceusage.serviceUsageConsumer (or a role containing serviceusage.services.use) to the service account at the project level.

title: Update pinned action versions for Node.js 24 deprecation
description:
GitHub Actions warns that actions/checkout and google-github-actions/auth are running on Node.js 20, which will be forced to Node.js 24 starting June 2, 2026. Update all pinned action SHAs across workflow files to versions that support Node.js 24 before the deadline.

Affected workflows: trigger-gcp-build.yml, consensus-validator.yml, and potentially others using actions/checkout@08eba0b27e820071cde6df949e0beb9ba4906955 or google-github-actions/auth pinned SHAs.

title: Consensus Validator depends on GCP build completing before Monday 06:00 UTC
description:
The consensus-validator workflow runs Monday at 06:00 UTC and requires results from both GitHub Actions and GCP Cloud Build. Currently GCP builds are broken (see serviceusage.services.use issue). Even after fixing IAM, the timing dependency is fragile: the trigger-gcp-build workflow fires after the Sunday GitHub build completes, and GCP Cloud Build must finish before Monday 06:00 UTC.

Consider adding a retry or delay mechanism in the consensus validator, or triggering it after both builds complete rather than on a fixed schedule.
